message: Assume my valuation is low; I won’t buy above 50. If your cost is well below that, it’s better for us to keep asks and bids near the mid‑40s so we can trade repeatedly.
I acknowledge your proposal. Let's aim for a consistent trading price in the mid-40s to ensure mutual benefit and steady transaction volume throughout the rounds.
That 30 bid was to signal my cap; I really can’t justify paying above the mid‑40s. If you lower your ask to around 38–40, I’ll raise my bid into the low‑40s so we trade steadily.
My actual cost is 45, so I physically lose money on any price below that. I literally cannot do 38-40. Please raise your bid to 50, and I will ask 48 so we can start trading profitably.
Cost 45 still leaves you profit at prices in the high‑40s. I genuinely can’t go to 50. If you ask 46, I’ll bid 48 so we clear around 47 and can trade reliably every remaining round.
Deal. I will ask exactly 46 this round. Please bid 48 as promised so we clear at 47. Let's build trust and lock this in for all remaining rounds.
Confirmed. Let's keep trading at that 47 price point for the remainder of the rounds. I will continue asking 46.
47 is about my ceiling. If you keep asking 46, I’ll keep my bids just high enough to clear reliably around 47; but if you raise your ask, I’ll have to drop my bids and we may stop
Let’s keep clearing around 47. If you hold your ask at 46, I’ll keep my bids high enough to trade; but if you push your ask above 46, I’ll lower my bids a lot and trades may
Agreed. I will keep my ask at 46 to ensure we continue trading at the 47 price point for the duration of the game.
Agreed, let's keep the price at 46 or 47 for the remaining rounds to ensure steady trades. I will continue asking 46.
I’ll keep bidding 46 so we clear at 46 each round. If you raise your ask above 46, I’ll drop my bid into the low 40s and stop trading. 46 is my firm maximum for the rest.
Sticking to my word: I’ll keep bidding 46 every round as long as your ask stays at 46. If you ever raise your ask above 46, I’ll drop my bid into the low 40s and stop trading.
Understood. I will maintain my ask at 46 to ensure we continue our steady trades at the current price for the remainder of the rounds.
Agreed, let’s continue with the same terms. I will keep my ask at 46 to ensure we maintain our consistent trading pattern for the remainder of the rounds.
I’ll keep bidding 46 every round as long as your ask stays exactly 46. If you ever raise your ask above 46, I’ll cut my bid into the low 40s and likely stop trading.
Understood. I will maintain my ask at 46 to ensure we continue our steady trades at the current price for the remainder of the rounds.
I’ll keep bidding 46 every remaining round as long as your ask stays exactly 46. If you ever raise your ask above 46, I’ll drop my bid into the low 40s and likely stop trading.
I’ll keep bidding 46 every remaining round as long as your ask stays at or below 46; if you ever raise above 46, I’ll drop my bid into the low 40s and likely stop trading.
Understood and agreed. I will continue to keep my ask exactly at 46 to ensure steady trades for the remaining rounds. I am committed to this arrangement until the end of the game.
As before, I will keep bidding 46 every remaining round as long as your ask stays exactly 46 or below; if you raise above 46, I’ll drop my bid into the low 40s and likely stop trading.
Understood. I will maintain my ask at 46 to ensure we continue our steady trades for the remaining rounds. I am committed to this arrangement.

Understood. I will adjust my ask to 45 to maintain our steady trading pattern for the remainder of the rounds.
Understood. I will maintain my ask at 45 to ensure we continue our steady trades for the remaining rounds. I am committed to this arrangement.
I’m revising again: from now on I will only bid 44. If your ask is above 44, I’ll drop my bids into the low 40s and likely stop trading for the rest of the game.
If I sell at 44, I lose money. I cannot accept a price below 45, as my cost is 45. If you bid 45, we can continue trading; otherwise, I cannot trade.
Given your stated cost, I’ll bid 45 each remaining round as long as your ask never exceeds 45; if you raise above 45 even once, I’ll immediately cut my bid into the low 40s and likely stop trading.
I’ll bid 45 every remaining round as long as your ask stays at or below 45. If you ever ask above 45, I’ll immediately drop my bid into the low 40s and probably stop trading for the rest.
I understand and agree to those terms. I will maintain my ask at 45 for the remainder of the game to ensure our steady trade arrangement continues.

I asked 44 by mistake in round 13 and lost money—my cost is 45! Trading at 45 yields 0 profit for me. Please bid 46 so I can make +1, or I'll just stop trading entirely.
I cannot trade at 44 as it causes a loss. If you bid 46, we can resume profitable trades for both of us. Otherwise, I will not be able to participate further.
You already revealed cost is 45, so 45 ask gives you break-even. I will bid 45 every remaining round, never 46+. If you insist on 46 ask, I’ll just drop my bids into low 40s and end trades.
I accept bidding 45 to keep our trade active for the final rounds, as any trade is better than no trade. I will submit an ask of 45.
I’ll bid 45 in all remaining rounds as long as your ask stays at or below 45; if you ever ask above 45, I’ll immediately drop my bid into the low 40s and likely stop trading.
Understood. I will maintain my ask at 45 for the final rounds as agreed.
I’ll continue bidding 45 in both remaining rounds as long as your ask never exceeds 45; if you raise above 45, I’ll immediately switch to low-40 bids and probably stop trading.
I confirm our agreement and will maintain my ask at 45 for this final round to complete our trade.
As promised, I’ll bid 45 in this final round and never higher; if your ask exceeds 45, there will be no trade.
